title: Schema for pointgroup data for Anyterial definition provider
description: JSON Schema for Anyterial crystallographic symmetry definitions for pointgroup data

id.title: ID
id.description:
A unique string referencing a specific entry in the database.

**Requirements/Conventions:**

- Taken together, the ID and entry type MUST uniquely identify the entry.
- Reasonably short IDs are encouraged and SHOULD NOT be longer than 255 characters.
- IDs MAY change over time.

type.title: type
type.description:
The name of the type of an entry.

**Requirements/Conventions:**

- MUST be an existing entry type.
- The entry of type <type> and ID <id> MUST be returned in response to a request for /<type>/<id> under the versioned or unversioned base URL serving the API.

immutable_id.title: immutable ID
immutable_id.description:
The entry's immutable ID (e.g., a UUID).

**Requirements/Conventions:**

- This is important for databases having preferred IDs that point to "the latest version" of a record, but still offer access to older variants.
- This ID maps to the version-specific record, in case it changes in the future.

last_modified.title: last modified
last_modified.description: Date and time representing when the entry was last modified.

character_table_complex.title: Character Table Complex
character_table_complex.$comment: Generated from data-generators JSON-LD fields without external definition URLs.
character_table_complex.description: Complex irreducible character table of the crystallographic point group. Rows correspond to complex irreducible representations and columns follow the order of `conjugacy_classes`.

items.description: One row of a point-group character table.

label_markup.title: String markups
label_markup.$comment: Reusable Anyterial definition for alternate display renderings of an ASCII/text string.
label_markup.description:
Alternate markup renderings of a string whose plain-text or ASCII value is provided by a sibling property.
The object is intended for display-oriented variants only; the corresponding unsuffixed sibling property remains the canonical plain string value.

**Requirements/Conventions**:

- It MUST be a dictionary with the following keys:

    - **html**: OPTIONAL; String.
      HTML rendering of the sibling string, using inline HTML elements where needed for typographic structure such as subscripts, superscripts, overlines, fractions, and line breaks.

    - **latex**: OPTIONAL; String.
      LaTeX rendering of the sibling string, suitable for use with a LaTeX or MathJax-like renderer.

    - **unicode**: OPTIONAL; String.
      Unicode rendering of the sibling string, using Unicode code points for display features where practical.

html.description: HTML rendering of the sibling string.

latex.description: LaTeX rendering of the sibling string.

unicode.description: Unicode rendering of the sibling string.

characters.description: Characters for the conjugacy classes in table order.

items.description: One complex character value represented as exact real and imaginary parts.

re.description: Exact real part represented as a string.

im.description: Exact imaginary part represented as a string.

basis_linear.description: Linear basis functions transforming as this representation.

items.description: One basis function.

basis_rotation.description: Rotational basis functions transforming as this representation.

items.description: One rotational basis function.

basis_quadratic.description: Quadratic basis functions transforming as this representation.

items.description: One quadratic basis function.

character_table_real.title: Character Table Real
character_table_real.$comment: Generated from data-generators JSON-LD fields without external definition URLs.
character_table_real.description: Real irreducible character table of the crystallographic point group. Rows correspond to real irreducible representations and columns follow the order of `conjugacy_classes`.

items.description: One row of a point-group character table.

label_markup.title: String markups
label_markup.$comment: Reusable Anyterial definition for alternate display renderings of an ASCII/text string.
label_markup.description:
Alternate markup renderings of a string whose plain-text or ASCII value is provided by a sibling property.
The object is intended for display-oriented variants only; the corresponding unsuffixed sibling property remains the canonical plain string value.

**Requirements/Conventions**:

- It MUST be a dictionary with the following keys:

    - **html**: OPTIONAL; String.
      HTML rendering of the sibling string, using inline HTML elements where needed for typographic structure such as subscripts, superscripts, overlines, fractions, and line breaks.

    - **latex**: OPTIONAL; String.
      LaTeX rendering of the sibling string, suitable for use with a LaTeX or MathJax-like renderer.

    - **unicode**: OPTIONAL; String.
      Unicode rendering of the sibling string, using Unicode code points for display features where practical.

html.description: HTML rendering of the sibling string.

latex.description: LaTeX rendering of the sibling string.

unicode.description: Unicode rendering of the sibling string.

characters.description: Characters for the conjugacy classes in table order.

items.description: One real character value represented as an exact integer.

basis_linear.description: Linear basis functions transforming as this representation.

items.description: One basis function.

basis_rotation.description: Rotational basis functions transforming as this representation.

items.description: One rotational basis function.

basis_quadratic.description: Quadratic basis functions transforming as this representation.

items.description: One quadratic basis function.

conjugacy_classes.title: Conjugacy Classes
conjugacy_classes.$comment: Generated from data-generators JSON-LD fields without external definition URLs.
conjugacy_classes.description: Conjugacy classes of a crystallographic point group. Each class lists its member operation indices, a representative operation, a conventional class label, and operation-type metadata used by the character tables.

items.description: One conjugacy class of a crystallographic point group.

ascii.description: Plain-text rendering intended for logs, command-line output, or compact display.

unicode.description: Unicode rendering of the same label.

latex.description: LaTeX rendering of the same label.

size.description: Number of point-group operations in the class.

members.description: Indices of operations belonging to this class.

items.description: Operation index.

representative.description: Index of a representative operation for the class.

op_type.description: Numeric operation type code of the representative.

items.description: One integer component.

crystal_system.title: Crystal System
crystal_system.$comment: Anyterial symmetry property definition with compatible external crystallographic definition IRIs.
crystal_system.description:
The crystal system of the space group or point group.

Values use the conventional crystallographic system names such as triclinic, monoclinic, orthorhombic, tetragonal, trigonal, hexagonal, and cubic.

hm_symbol.title: Hermann-Mauguin Symbol
hm_symbol.$comment: Generated from data-generators JSON-LD fields without external definition URLs.
hm_symbol.description: Hermann-Mauguin point-group symbol used as the key and display symbol for a point-group record.

is_centrosymmetric.title: Is Centrosymmetric
is_centrosymmetric.$comment: Generated from data-generators JSON-LD fields without external definition URLs.
is_centrosymmetric.description: Boolean flag indicating whether the point group contains inversion symmetry.

laue_class.title: Laue Class
laue_class.$comment: Anyterial symmetry property definition with compatible external crystallographic definition IRIs.
laue_class.description:
The Laue class associated with the space group or point group.

The Laue class groups point groups that become equivalent when inversion symmetry is included.

n_conjugacy_classes.title: N Conjugacy Classes
n_conjugacy_classes.$comment: Generated from data-generators JSON-LD fields without external definition URLs.
n_conjugacy_classes.description: Number of conjugacy classes in the crystallographic point group.

order.title: Order
order.$comment: Generated from data-generators JSON-LD fields without external definition URLs.
order.description: Order of the point group, i.e. the number of operations in the finite point group.

schoenflies.title: Schoenflies Symbol
schoenflies.$comment: Anyterial symmetry property definition with compatible external crystallographic definition IRIs.
schoenflies.description:
The Schoenflies symbol for the space-group type.

The ASCII form follows the CIF convention for `_space_group.name_Schoenflies`, using a period to separate the Schoenflies point-group symbol from the superscript index.

symops.title: Symmetry operations
symops.$comment: Anyterial property definition using the common reusable op object definition.
symops.description:
Full list of symmetry-operation descriptors for a point group.
Each list member is a `op` object as defined by `/properties/symmetry/op`.
For point-group operations, generated data currently uses `matrix` and `type`.

**Requirements/Conventions**:

- It MUST be a list of dictionaries.
- Each dictionary MUST follow the schema inherited from `/properties/symmetry/op`.

items.title: Operation
items.$comment: Reusable Anyterial definition for one classified crystallographic operation descriptor.
items.description:
A classified crystallographic operation acting within one coordinate setting.
The affine map itself is stored in the embedded `affine_transformation` field.
The remaining fields classify the operation crystallographically, for example by rotation type, axis, sense, and screw or glide component.

**Requirements/Conventions**:

- It MUST be a dictionary with the following keys:

    - **affine\_transformation**: REQUIRED; Dictionary.
      Exact affine map for the operation.
      It MUST follow `/defs/v0.1/properties/symmetry/affine_transformation`.

    - **rot\_type**: OPTIONAL; String.
      Crystallographic operation-type label for the linear part, such as `1`, `-1`, `2`, `m`, `-3`, `4`, `-4`, `6`, or `-6`.

    - **type**: OPTIONAL; Integer.
      Legacy numeric operation-type code used by point-group operation descriptors.

    - **axis**: OPTIONAL; List of 3 Integers.
      Operation axis or invariant direction using the integer-vector convention returned by the generator.

    - **sense**: OPTIONAL; Integer.
      Rotation sense/sign convention returned by the generator; `0` is used when no handed rotation sense is applicable.

    - **screw\_glide**: OPTIONAL; List of 3 Fractions (String).
      Screw-axis or glide-plane component associated with a space-group affine operation.

    - **origin\_shift**: OPTIONAL; List of 3 Fractions (String).
      Origin shift associated with the screw/glide decomposition of a space-group affine operation.

    - **is\_proper**: OPTIONAL; Boolean.
      States whether the linear operation is proper, i.e., whether its determinant is +1.

affine_transformation.title: Affine transformation
affine_transformation.$comment: Reusable Anyterial definition for the pure affine-map part of crystallographic transformation records.
affine_transformation.description:
One exact affine transformation acting on fractional crystallographic coordinates.
The transformation is represented by a 3 by 3 matrix and a 3-vector, both serialized with exact string entries.
Parent properties define the coordinate convention and semantic role of the transformation, for example whether it is an operation within one setting, a setting transform, a subgroup embedding, or a normalizer representative.

**Requirements/Conventions**:

- It MUST be a dictionary with the following keys:

    - **matrix**: REQUIRED; Exact 3x3 matrix.
      Matrix part of the affine transformation.
      It MUST be represented as a list of three row lists, each containing three exact rational entries represented as strings.

    - **vector**: REQUIRED; List of 3 Fractions (String).
      Translation or origin-shift vector of the affine transformation in fractional coordinates.

    - **xyz**: OPTIONAL; String.
      Coordinate expression for the affine transformation in `x,y,z` notation when available.

    - **det**: OPTIONAL; Integer.
      Determinant of `matrix` when the generator emits it.

    - **is\_orthogonal**: OPTIONAL; Boolean.
      Whether `matrix` is orthogonal in the exact representation used by the generator.

matrix.description: Exact 3 by 3 matrix part of the affine transformation.

items.description: One row of the exact 3 by 3 matrix.

items.title: fraction
items.description: A fraction represented as a string.

vector.description: Exact fractional-coordinate vector part of the affine transformation.

items.title: fraction
items.description: A fraction represented as a string.

xyz.description: Coordinate expression for the affine transformation in `x,y,z` notation.

det.description: Determinant of the matrix part when emitted by the generator.

is_orthogonal.description: Whether the matrix part is orthogonal.

rot_type.description: Symbolic crystallographic operation-type label for the linear part.

axis.description: Integer-vector axis or invariant-direction descriptor for the operation.

items.description: One integer component of the axis vector.

sense.description: Rotation sense/sign convention returned by the generator.

screw_glide.description: Screw-axis or glide-plane component represented exactly as a list of fraction strings.

items.title: fraction
items.description: A fraction represented as a string.

origin_shift.description: Origin-shift descriptor represented exactly as a list of fraction strings.

items.title: fraction
items.description: A fraction represented as a string.

is_proper.description: Whether the linear operation is proper.

schoenflies_markup.title: Schoenflies symbol markups
schoenflies_markup.$comment: Anyterial symmetry property definition inheriting the common string markup object.
schoenflies_markup.description:
Display-oriented renderings of the Schoenflies symbol in `schoenflies`.
The plain string value is stored in the corresponding unsuffixed property; this object only provides alternate markup forms for display.

html.description: HTML rendering of the sibling string.

latex.description: LaTeX rendering of the sibling string.

unicode.description: Unicode rendering of the sibling string.
